data.sentence: Alicia, hey, thanks for making time today — I know you mentioned earlier this week that things have been pretty hectic over there.

Yeah, hectic is honestly an understatement, Maria. March has just been — it's been a lot. But yes, I'm glad we could get this on the calendar because there are some things I really do need to talk through with you.

Of course, absolutely. That's exactly why I wanted to set this up. I want to make sure we're being a good partner to Summit Trust and, um, if there are concerns on your end I'd rather hear them directly than not at all.

Right, and I appreciate that. I do. Look, I'll be honest with you — our renewal is coming up and I'm getting pressure from my CISO and from our compliance team and everyone is sort of, uh, looking at the platform and asking questions that I'm finding it hard to answer right now.
Okay. And I want to hear all of it. Can you start with — like, what's the top of the list for you? What's causing the most pain?
Honestly? Identity. Aegis Identity. That's the one that's causing me the most grief right now with my internal stakeholders.
Okay, tell me more. Is it a specific feature, is it performance, is it the implementation — like what are you running into?

It's kind of... a few things layered on top of each other, which is what makes it frustrating. So, we have about, I want to say, around 2,400 employees now after the merger, right? And we are supposed to be using Aegis Identity for SSO across our core banking applications and MFA for anything that touches customer data.
Right, yep, that's how the implementation was scoped.

So the MFA piece — we're still seeing inconsistent enforcement. Like, there are users who are getting through to sensitive systems without being prompted, and when I bring that to your support team the answer I get is basically 'check your policy config' and we've checked it, Maria. We've checked it multiple times. Our IT team has gone through this and we're not getting a real resolution.
That's — yeah, I hear you and that's not okay. Inconsistent MFA enforcement in financial services, I mean, that's not just a technical inconvenience, that's a real risk exposure for you.

Exactly. And my CISO sees it that way. He's basically told me that if we can't demonstrate consistent MFA enforcement by end of Q2, we need to be having a different conversation about the platform entirely.

Okay. And I want to get you a proper answer on that — not a 'check your config' answer, like an actual engineering-level look at what's happening. Can you tell me which applications specifically are showing the bypass issue?

It's been showing up mostly in our loan origination system and our internal reporting portal. Those are the two that we've flagged. And look, our IT director sent a ticket in — I want to say it was maybe ten days ago? — and the last update we got was five days ago saying it was under investigation. That's it. Just 'under investigation.'

Ten days with a five-day-old update on something like that is not acceptable. I'm going to personally escalate that ticket today. I'll find out exactly where it sits and who owns it and I'll get back to you by end of day with an actual status.
I appreciate that. I really do. But Maria, this isn't the first time we've had to escalate something that should have been handled at the support level, and that pattern is part of the problem.

You're right. And I'm not going to sit here and make excuses for that, because you're right. That pattern — that's something I need to take back internally and make sure we're fixing how we're handling Summit Trust's tickets. You're a strategic account and you should be treated that way.

And I want to be fair — it's not all bad. The Comply module has been... actually things have been okay there, better than expected, and I heard there's a new version coming? We got some communication about that.

Yes! Yeah, Comply v2 just — well, it's actually going GA on April 7th, so pretty soon. On-demand reporting for SOC 2, PCI DSS, HIPAA, ISO 27001, all in one place. Given your compliance requirements I think that's going to be a pretty big deal for your team.

Yeah, that actually does sound useful, especially around PCI DSS. That one's always a scramble for us. But I don't want to get distracted by that because the Identity stuff is really where my head is right now.
No, totally fair. Let's stay on Identity. So MFA enforcement is one thing — you said there are a few layers. What else?

The SSO session management. We're getting reports from users — and this has been happening more frequently in the last few weeks — where sessions are timing out mid-workflow. Like someone is in the middle of processing a loan application and they get kicked out and have to re-authenticate. And in a banking environment that's not just annoying, it's disruptive, and it erodes trust in the platform internally.
Have you — is the timeout behavior consistent with what was configured in your session policy, or is it happening earlier than expected?
Earlier than expected. We have it set to 60 minutes of inactivity but people are getting timed out at like 20, 25 minutes. And they're not inactive — they're working.

Okay, that sounds like it could be a session token refresh issue — um, there's been a known behavior in certain configurations where... actually, I don't want to speculate because I might get this wrong. I want to pull in one of our Identity specialists on this one and have them look at your specific config. Would you be open to a technical call next week?

I'm open to it, but I need to be candid with you, Maria. We've done the 'let's schedule a technical call' thing before and sometimes those calls don't produce outcomes. So I'm willing to do it but I need there to be accountability on your side that something actually changes.

That's completely fair and I hear you. How about this — I'll commit to sending you a written summary after that call with specific action items, owners, and timelines. Not just meeting notes, like actual accountable deliverables. Would that help?

That would help, yes. And honestly... look, I might as well be transparent with you about something. We've been having some conversations with VaultEdge. Our CISO initiated it, not me, but they've been talking to us about their identity and access management capabilities and I'm being asked to evaluate them.

I appreciate you telling me that directly. I really do. And I'm not going to panic and start telling you VaultEdge is terrible — that's not helpful. What I do want to understand is what's driving that conversation on his end. Is it specifically the issues you've described, or is there something else?

Mostly it's the issues I've described. He feels like — and I think I agree with him — that the IAM piece of what we're doing doesn't feel enterprise-grade. Like, it feels like there are rough edges that shouldn't be there for a company our size, in our industry, with our risk profile.

Yeah. And I think that's fair feedback, and it's feedback I need to take seriously. Can I ask — and you don't have to answer this — is the VaultEdge conversation at a stage where it's like an active evaluation with demos and pricing, or is it more exploratory at this point?

It's... honestly somewhere in between. We've had an intro call with them and we're supposed to see a demo next week. But I want to be clear — I'm not trying to use this as leverage. I'm telling you because I think you should know and because I want to give Aegis a fair chance to address these things.

And I genuinely appreciate that. Alicia, what would it take — like if you could paint the picture for me — what would it look like for Aegis Identity to be working in a way where this conversation isn't happening? What does good look like for you?

Consistent MFA enforcement with no exceptions — and I can verify it. Session timeouts that actually match our configured policy. And honestly, a support experience where I'm not having to escalate everything through you personally. Those are the three things. They're not crazy asks.

They're not crazy asks at all. They're baseline things that should be working and I'm not going to pretend otherwise. I'm going to take everything from today and work with our Identity team and our support leadership to put together a formal remediation plan. Not a PowerPoint, like an actual plan with dates.

Okay. And I want to — I do want to mention one more thing, and I know this is a separate topic but it's still in the back of my mind. That outage earlier this month. The Detect one. That was six hours of no visibility and I had to explain that to my CISO and that was not a fun conversation.
Yeah. The March outage — I owe you a proper conversation about that. We've been proactive with some customers but I should have reached out to you directly and sooner, and I didn't, and that's on me.
What happened exactly? The communication we got was pretty vague.

So there was a cascading failure in the event processing pipeline — it was a single point of failure in event ingestion that triggered the whole thing. It's been fixed. We've added redundant processing nodes and implemented a circuit breaker pattern so it can't cascade that way again. But I know 'we fixed it' doesn't undo the six hours and it doesn't undo the conversation you had to have.

No, it doesn't. And I guess what concerns me is — how do I know this kind of thing isn't going to happen again? With Identity, with Detect, with something else? That's the underlying trust issue, Maria.

That's the real question and I don't want to give you a fluffy answer. What I can do is get you a formal post-incident report on the Detect outage and I can also ask our engineering team to walk you through the architecture changes if that would give your CISO more confidence. Would that be worth doing?
Yes. Actually yes, I think that would be useful. If he can hear it from an engineer rather than from a slide deck, that carries more weight with him.

Okay. So let me summarize what I'm taking away from today so we're aligned. I'm escalating the MFA ticket today and getting you a real status by end of day. I'm setting up a technical Identity call for next week with written action items after. I'm getting you a post-incident report on the Detect outage and exploring an engineering briefing for your CISO. And I'm putting together a formal remediation plan for the Identity issues overall. Does that capture it?

That captures it. And Maria — I want to be clear, I'm not trying to be difficult. I want this to work. We have a lot invested in this platform and switching costs are real. But I need to be able to defend this decision internally and right now that's hard.

I understand. And the fact that you're telling me all of this instead of just quietly going to VaultEdge — I take that seriously and I'm not going to waste the opportunity. You'll hear from me today on the ticket status.
Okay. Good. Let's do that. And Maria — thanks for not being defensive about any of this. I know it's not the easiest call to be on.
Not the easiest, no, but it's the right one to have. I'll be in touch later today. Thanks Alicia.
